steps.title: Read the roots from the factored numerator
steps.content: Shortcut (Vieta's): The fraction equals zero only when its numerator equals zero. The numerator $(x-3)(x-8)$ is already factored, giving roots $x = 3$ and $x = 9$. Wait — $(x-8) = 0$ gives $x = 8$. Roots are $x = 3$ and $x = 8$. The excluded value $x = 2$ is neither, so both roots are valid.

steps.title: Sum of roots directly
steps.content: Sum $= 3 + 8 = 11$. By Vieta's, the numerator expands to $x^2 - 11x + 24$, confirming sum $= -(-11)/1 = 11$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
